results.text:
A Kombucha So Good, It Might Convert Kombucha-Skeptics — The Candidly

Sign In My Account

# A Kombucha So Good, It Might Convert Kombucha-Skeptics

A Kombucha So Good, It Might Convert Kombucha-Skeptics

WHAT: Health-Ade

WHY: I mean, we are a wellness site, so we have to address kombucha.

Some people love it, many people loathe it. The probiotic benefits have been debated. The taste has inspired one of the best memes of all time. Fine, it’s The Best meme of all time.

Whether you love kombucha, have never tried it, or just drink it for the probiotics, Health-Ade is undoubtedly the best brand. It’s USDA organic, non-GMO, vegan, and gluten free. Plus, it’s made in small batches with cold-pressed juices.

Unlike brands which rely on intensely-strong ginger flavors to make it seem “natural” and “healthy,” Health-Ade’s many flavors are usually fruity-yet-subtle. Pink Lady Apple is the classic, but Cherry Berry might be The Best. The fizz is also pleasantly subtle, instead of painfully sharp like most store-bought kombucha.

Health-Ade is so good, we’ve actually witnessed it turn kombucha-skeptics into believers.

PRICE: $44 for a 12-pack

At The Candidly, we try a lot of stuff so you don’t have to. We only recommend things we truly love, and that we think you’ll love, too. All products are chosen independently by our creative team, and all details reflect the price and availability of products at the time of publication. If you buy something we link to, The Candidly may earn a commission. We have to eat.

results.title: Health-Ade Holiday Cheers Kombucha, Our Honest Review | Sporked
results.author: Gwynedd Stuart
results.text:
Health-Ade Holiday Cheers Kombucha, Our Honest Review | Sporked 

 

 

# Will Health-Ade Holiday Cheers Kombucha Make Your Spirits Bright?

 

Credit: Liv Averett / Health-Ade Kombucha

Copy this link to share with your friends!

`
 https://sporked.com/article/health-ade-holiday-cheers-kombucha-review/ `

 

I like the idea of a holiday-themed kombucha. The days are short and hectic and you’re eating junk for two months straight, so if you buy into the gut health benefits of probiotic bevs, you could presumably avoid a lot of gastro-intestinal angst by sipping a festive kombucha instead of, say, a big ol’ mug of eggnog.

## Videos by Sporked

Health-Ade Holiday Cheers kombucha purportedly celebrates the holiday flavors “of ginger, vanilla, and allspice,” which, if you’re not familiar, tastes like a mixture of nutmeg, black pepper, cinnamon and clove. Allspice is essentially a peppercorn, but the label on the bottle features two anthropomorphic spices—nutmeg and star anise. Or maybe it’s just a star. Who knows. Anyway, we’re here to tell you whether this new kombucha flavor is a holiday must or a holiday bust.

New Product!

Health-Ade Holiday Cheers Kombucha

Health-Ade

Giving yourself the gift of gut health is such a lovely idea, but we’re here to tell you whether this stuff actually tastes good. First of all, this does look like a gift. The brown apothecary-style bottle that Health-Ade comes in makes it feel fancier than kombucha that comes in a regular bottle or can. So that’s a good start!

Pros: We really like Health-Ade kombucha. In fact, their Pink Lady Apple flavor is our top pick for the best tasting kombucha of them all. This Health-Ade flavor doesn’t make as much of an impression, but it does certainly taste like ginger, as promised.

Cons: Did I taste allspice or vanilla? Not really. Maybe the tart ginger and fermented tang of the kombucha itself was a little bit rounder because of the vanilla, but I could hardly detect it. And if there is festive holiday spice in this, it was pretty much lost on me. This tastes like a very standard ginger kombucha, which is something Health-Ade already makes. I didn’t try this alongside Health-Ade Lemon-Ginger kombucha, but I would be very surprised if there were a big difference in their flavor profiles. Health-Ade Holiday Cheers tastes good—really, it’s fine—but it’s not going to have you singing “Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer” at the top of your lungs.

Rating:

7/10

Sporks

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Review: A Thorough Analysis | 33rd Square
results.author: Terry Williams
results.text:
Health-Ade Kombucha Review: A Thorough Analysis | 33rd Square

## About Health-Ade Kombucha

Health-Ade was founded in 2012 by husband and wife team Justin and Daina Trout along with their best friend Vanessa Drew. The concept originated when the trio started selling homemade kombucha at local Los Angeles farmer‘s markets. Their creation was a huge hit with customers who loved the tasty flavors and focus on using wholesome, organic ingredients.

Propelled by this initial success, the friends made the leap into larger-scale production and distribution. By 2013, Health-Ade Kombucha was available in retail locations across Southern California including Whole Foods and Bristol Farms.

Over the next few years, the brand‘s distribution footprint grew rapidly to encompass natural grocers, conventional retailers, cafes and restaurants nationwide. They now boast over 200 employees and sell millions of cases annually, making Health-Ade a category leader in the kombucha space.

So what‘s behind this impressive growth? Let‘s analyze what‘s made the Health-Ade brand so popular and why it stands out from the ever-expanding array of kombucha options on the market.

## The Health Benefits of Kombucha

As a probiotic-rich, fermented tea drink, kombucha has gained immense popularity as a health food and wellness product. Health-Ade leans into these science-backed benefits in their brand messaging and product innovation.

Research has shown kombucha can positively impact digestive and gut health. A literature review in the Journal of Food Science found drinking kombucha regularly can reduce LDL cholesterol levels, blood pressure and other cardiometabolic risk factors. The fermentation process creates organic acids with antioxidant properties while probiotics work to balance gut flora.

Additional studies point to kombucha‘s antimicrobial qualities which may boost immunity. Compounds in the tea, like polyphenols and vitamin C, have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects. The probiotics and vitamins also energize and protect cells.

There is also early evidence showing consumption of some compounds found in kombucha could potentially have anticancer effects. More research is still needed in humans.

While findings are preliminary, the cumulative research makes a compelling case for integrating kombucha like Health-Ade into a healthy lifestyle. Of course, check with your doctor before making any major dietary changes.

## Examining Health-Ade Kombucha

Now that we‘ve covered the basics on kombucha and its wellness benefits, let’s analyze Health-Ade’s specific brand, ingredients, and manufacturing process through an evidence-based lens.

### Health-Ade‘s Pros

- Give-back philosophy: Partners with nonprofits and community organizations
- Ethical and eco-conscious practices: Fair worker wages, reduced environmental impact initiatives
- Range of unique flavors: Over 25 options including seasonal and limited-edition kombuchas
- Natural fermentation process: Health-Ade le

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Cayenne Cleanse Review
results.text:
Health-Ade Kombucha Cayenne Cleanse Review

Skip to Content

Tyler Joe

Every item on this page was chosen by an ELLE editor. We may earn commission on some of the items you choose to buy.

## THE EDITOR Market editor Justine Carreon

## THE LOVE Health-Ade Kombucha Cayenne Cleanse, $24 for 6

TRY IT

Sit me down at dinner and regular tap water is not enough. I am a big fan of beverages. I’m also a sucker. I don’t know what "gut health" is or whether or not I should be paying attention to it, but I believe in the power of Kombucha. It might have to do with a disgusting habit of eating rice with vinegar as a kid, but ‘buch is something I drink often and without fault.

I’m far from what I think of as a stereotypical Kombucha drinker (i.e., I don’t live off a plant-based diet). Instead, I work in a corporate office and buy it on the reg from my local bodega because honestly, I just like how it tastes. As I’ve matured—pronounced in that spine-tinglingly creepy way—my tolerance for sugar has dropped. A childhood of sugary soda developed into finding the taste of Coke akin to maple syrup. I dabble with sparking drinks, but even ELLE.com-backed Spindrift is too sweet for me.

Tyler Joe

#ELLEloves is a daily place to share the things ELLE editors love. Because sharing is caring.

When my friend Devon, who is unsurprisingly vegan, introduced me to Health-Ade, I was hooked. I stick to the Cayenne Cleanse or Ginger Lemon flavors because I’m a beverage sadist who loves a carbonated burn. It makes me feel like I’m chugging the entire wellness industry in one gulp, negating the Oreos and chicken tenders I eat regularly. I treat it as a magical elixir solving my dietary faults. My stomach really does feel better after I’ve emptied the amber-tinged glass (it looks like it belongs in a medical cabinet). If that’s the placebo effect at work, call me double blind, pass me another bottle of fermented tea, and leave me in ignorant ‘buch bliss.

Try it

Tyler Joe

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Review - Must Read This Before Buying
results.text:
Health-Ade Kombucha Review - Must Read This Before Buying 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Wellness

# Health-Ade Kombucha Review

Table of Contents

## About Health-Ade

If you’re interested in wellness and nutrition, then you’re bound to have heard of kombucha. This super healthy drink has become a craze in the last few years, with consumers loving its delicious taste and nutritional benefits. However, as studies note, not all kombucha is the same.

Health-Ade is passionate about making the best-tasting, highest-quality kombucha using only natural ingredients. And, despite there being quite a few kombucha brands on the market now, this one seems to stand out from the crowd.

Health-Ade was founded in 2012 in Los Angeles, CA by a husband and wife team, Justin and Daina Trout, and their best friend, Vanessa Drew. The kombucha was first available at LA farmer’s markets, but the brand quickly expanded to selling in stores and via its website.

The founders had a dream to make incredibly tasting, healthy, and super high-quality kombucha. With customers’ wellness in mind, the brand focuses on being a “champion of the happiest and healthiest you.”

Today, Health-Ade boasts 29 yummy flavors of kombucha across four different collections. While some products are meant to be enjoyed right when you open the bottle or can, the company also offers mixers, which can add a dose of nutrition to your favorite alcoholic or non-alcoholic drink.

Along with tasting amazing, all Health-Ade kombucha is certified vegan and uses non-GMO and USDA organic ingredients. The company also supports ethical conditions for all workers involved in the production and distribution of its products.

 

Additionally, the brand ensures it reaches out to the community. Recently, it has teamed up with the Garden School Foundation to help underprivileged children learn about gardening and nutrition.

So, is this the brand you should turn to for your next bottle of nutritious goodness? This Health-Ade kombucha review will help you figure that out. I’ll look at the company, its bestsellers, customer ratings, and more, so stay tuned.

shop now

## Pros and Cons

### Pros

1. High-Quality Ingredients: Health-Ade prides itself on using high-quality, organic ingredients in their kombucha. They use real fruits, herbs, and teas, and avoid artificial flavors or preservatives.
2. Fermented and Probiotic-Rich: Kombucha is a fermented beverage that contains live cultures and probiotics, which are beneficial for gut health. Health-Ade kombucha undergoes a natural fermentation process, resulting in a probiotic-rich product.
3. Flavor Variety: Health-Ade offers a wide range of flavors, catering to different taste preferences. From classic flavors like Ginger-Lemon to unique combinations like Pink Lady Apple and Bubbly Rose, there is a flavor for everyone.
4. Transparency and Sustainability: Health-Ade emphasizes transparency in their manufacturing processes. They provide detailed information about their ingr

results.title: Health-Ade Kombucha Holiday Cheers Review | PS Fitness
results.author: Lauren Pardee
results.text:
Health-Ade Kombucha Holiday Cheers Review | PS Fitness

While each product featured is independently selected by our editors, we may include paid promotion. If you buy something through our links, we may earn commission. Read more about our Product Review Guidelines here.

Every December, I begin my holiday kitchen residency — a month spent living between my kitchen island and stove, creating everything from gingerbread cookies to peppermint bark to spiked eggnog. I've even dedicated a Christmas playlist and apron to the occasion.

Despite my excitement, my stomach doesn't find this to be the most wonderful month of the year. Everything tastes great going in (except for an occasional burnt sugar cookie!), but one person can only indulge in so many sweet treats before their body starts to speak up.

So I try to supplement my baking hobby with gut-healthy foods and drinks, like Health-Ade's Holiday Cheers Kombucha($48 for a 12-pack).

Health-Ade's Holiday Cheers KombuchaFrom$48$48 at health-ade.com

I'm not claiming kombucha can cure stomachaches, but the fermented drink has been shown to improve digestion, decrease bloating, and help with gut inflammation— and I feel it works for me.

While I'm picky about kombucha (I find some taste way too vinegary!), Health-Ade's Holiday Cheers flavor passes my taste test. Ginger is the star of this show, but notes of vanilla, allspice, and chocolate lend the blend a sweet and warm balance. The recipe will complement most holiday treats, and the green-and-red packaging works with my kitchen aesthetic, too.

I've even been known to bring Health-Ade's Holiday Cheers Kombucha to holiday parties on nights that I'm assigned as the designated driver. The crisp, bubbly flavors are hardly bland and actually make for a great conversation starter. And although all gatherings are on Zoom this year, don't be surprised if I (virtually!) cheers to the season with my favorite glass bottle in hand despite not needing to drive — it's that good.

Dry January? Improved Gut Health with Health-Ade Kombucha | Health-Ade — duuude | Only the Good Stuff- Reviews, Must Grabs, and Deals

# Dry January? Improve Gut Health in 2023 with Health-Ade Kombucha

Jan 1

### Happy New Year [+Gut]

If you are like us, heading into a New Year has us focusing on our health and ways to improve it in 2023. Our friends at Health-Ade, you know, the makers of delicious beverages that support a happy and healthy gut, are celebrating this coming new year with “A Different Kind of Feel-Good.” As we head into January, many folks are celebrating Dry January. What the heck is “Dry January?”

Dry January started in 2012 as an initiative by Alcohol Change UK, a British charity, to “ditch the hangover, reduce the waistline, and save some serious money by giving up alcohol for 31 days.” Millions of people now take part in the challenge, with more Americans taking notice each year.

As Dry January picks up steam on the shelves and in our bellies, so does a healthy, gut-friendly, bubbly drink called Kombucha.

So who says Dry January has to be boring? Let’s cheers to feeling good from the inside out this coming year! Sip on something equal parts fun, feel-good, and functional. Day to night, all night to the next morning…wherever the party takes you, Drink for a Different Kind of Feel-Good. Our crew got our hands (and gut) onto some of Health-Ade’s Kombucha for the ultimate taste test ahead of this year’s Dry January. Let’s pop some bottle shall we?

### INITIAL IMPRESSIONS

It is important to first start off with this- our crew usually hates bubbly-like waters. We have tried several over the years, we have tasted all sorts of “gut healthy drinks” and have spit them all out. Yes, hate is a strong word but we had to be real with how finicky we are with drinks. Until now. Spoiler Alert for the post: This Kombucha is the best gut-health drink we have ever tasted.

Trust Your Gut.

### Kombucha FAQ

#### What is Kombucha?

Kombucha is fermented tea, and contains probiotics and healthy organic acids. It has been consumed for thousands of years across countless cultures as a beverage that promotes health, and has recently been on a major rise in the US.

#### What does it taste like?

It tastes sweet yet has a little tartness to it as well. Toss in some flavors like Pomegranate and Bubbly Rose, it can taste like a cocktail

#### Why Kombucha?

Kombucha helps you gut by delivering prebiotics, probiotics, and fermented foods.

Kombucha is: Tea, water, sugar and a culture (the collection of microorganisms responsible for fermentation) are all you need to make kombucha! At Health-Ade we use a blend of green and black tea, and add cold-pressed juice from organic produce for that extra fresh flavor.

Health-Ade offers quite the range of Kombucha flavors with no unnecessary fillers or hard-to-pronounce ingredients. Just clean Tea, water, sugar and a culture (the collection of microorganisms responsible for fermentation).

Our initial duuude

results.title: Review: New Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors, Ranked
results.text:
Review: New Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors, Ranked

# Review: New Health-Ade Kombucha Flavors, Ranked

By Melissa Gaman Feb. 22, 2024 2:15 pm EST

Melissa Gaman/Tasting Table

Kombucha is a bubbly, fermented, tea-based drink that has had a rapid rise in popularity due to its health benefits, the growing number of brands stocking supermarket shelves, and the ever-expanding choice of flavors. Whether made at home or commercially, the process is pretty similar. Tea (black, green, or both) is mixed with sugar, water, and a symbiotic culture of bacteria and yeast. It ferments in two stages, resulting in a probiotic drink that is sour, sweet, carbonated, and packed with live, active cultures. Making kombucha yourself takes time, experimentation, and some equipment, so many turn to prepared kombucha, and that's where Health-Ade comes in.

Health-Ade has been leading the pack in the kombucha market in the US since its launch in 2012. The brand has risen from a small company selling at farmer's markets to a nationally available brand in a wide variety of flavors. Health-Ade is easily recognizable thanks to the signature squat brown glass bottles, though cans of kombucha are now also available. In addition to a core group of flavors, Health-Ade introduces new flavors and limited-time seasonal offerings throughout the year. Recently, Health-Ade launched three tropical flavors, and we couldn't resist giving them a taste to see how they stack up. As a long-time fan of kombucha, I was looking forward to assessing the overall flavor of each drink in addition to how well the flavor matched the name of the drink.

Some recommendations are based on first-hand impressions of promotional materials and products provided by the manufacturer/distributor/etc.

## What are the new Health-Ade Flavors?

Melissa Gaman/Tasting Table

Health-Ade recently launched new tropical flavors that can be found individually online, or in stores, or as The Tropical Variety Pack on the Health-Ade website. The kombucha trio includes a new permanent flavor, an old favorite with a new name, and a new seasonal flavor. Health-Ade rates the trio tasting notes on a scale as 3 out of 3 for fruity flavors, 2 out of 3 for tangy flavors, and 1 out of 3 for floral flavors, making it a refreshing combination for someone looking for a fruit-forward sweet-tart balance.

Tropical Pineapple is not a new flavor for Health-Ade, but it now has a new name. Previously called Tropical Punch, the main flavor profile is the same with cold-pressed pineapple juice accented with mango and orange. Guava Dragon Fruit debuted as a seasonal flavor and was so popular that it has become a permanent flavor. The kombucha is flavored with guava and dragon fruit purees for a sweet-tart, floral combo. Lastly, the seasonal offering of the group is Mango Lemonade. Sweet mango puree balances tart lemon juice for a tropical twist on a classic drink. Each flavor is 80 calories for a 16-ounce bottle and contains between 7% and 10% jui

results.title: Ultimate Kombucha Taste Test Health-Ade Bubbly Rose Kombucha – [RVJedeye]
results.author: View all posts by RVJEDEYE
results.text:
Ultimate Kombucha Taste Test Health-Ade Bubbly Rose Kombucha – [RVJedeye]

GREEN BAY, WI – (UPDATE) I keep saying to myself, “Self… there is GOING to be a flavor of Kombucha you’re not going to like. I know it’s coming.”

And I’m going to be honest with you, in this week’s “Ultimate Kombucha Taste Test” brand & flavor, “Health-Ade Bubbly Rosen Kombucha,” the amazing streak continues I a BIG way!

Once again, if you’re just joining in on the fun, this whole taste test challenge came from my daughter sending me some Kombucha for Father’s Day Her & her significant other, Trevor aka “T-Man” told me I should do a taste test as I was going through the beverages and review them, so that’s what I’ve been doing.

This week’s flavor shocked be folks… I’m not going to lie. When I pulled out the Health – Ade Kombucha Bubbly Rose I did not have high hopes for it. It’s made with Hawthorn Berry, Mangosteen and Rose Petals… It didn’t really sound like a combination that interest me that much. However, Health-Ade was a totally new brand for me so I didn’t know what to expect at all.

This one blew me away! I could have easily had a second bottle… right away! That’s how good it was. It’s not too sweet and there is not a ton of a vinegary taste at all. In fact, like“Clear Mind,” by Brew Dr, I think the blended Kombucha’s seem to have a bit of an edge. I mean I love fruit flavor and tropical fruit flavor’s are at the head of the class, however the blends are knocking it out of the park. Fresh over ice and right from the bottle, this one is near perfection!

You can read the label & get your critical information off of it. 80 calories and 14g of sugars & carbohydrates are at 18g.

So, for me the overall “UKTT” score this week is a 9.8… Yep, almost perfection:

| Date: | Maker: | Flavor | Score: |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| July 15, 2020 | Kevita | Raspberry Lemon | 7 |
| July 22, 2020 | Gt’s Synergy | Trilogy | 9 |
| July 29, 2020 | Brew Dr | Clear Mind | 9.5 |
| August 5, 2020 | Fresh Thyme | Honeycrisp Apple | 9 |
| August12, 2020 | Gt’s Synergy | Guava Goddess | 8.5 |
| August 19, 2020 | Health-Ade | Bubbly Rose | 9.8 |

If you want to see the videos for the mini series, that I did before, here is the link to the Complete Taste Test Challenge Series!

Until next week! Enjoy and comment below!

If you’re looking for a refreshing kombucha drink that supports a happy gut, you’ve probably come across Health-Ade Kombucha. Whether you’re a fan of classic fermented tea or exploring new kombucha flavors, their pink lady apple variety offers the perfect balance of sweetness and tang. But is it worth the hype? Let’s find out!
